message.content:
Heydon, great observation -- and good news, this is already implemented in the current codebase. The route handler in `src/routes/register.rs` has exactly the translation layer you're describing: **Already in place (lines 61-82, 90-106):** - `DuplicateEmail` -> "Unable to create account. If you already have an account, try signing in." (generic, no account enumeration) - `InvalidEmail(Empty)` -> "Enter your email address" (via `user_facing_email_error()`) - `InvalidEmail(MissingAtSymbol)` -> "Enter a valid email address, like name@example.com" - `InvalidPassword(TooShort)` -> "Your password needs at least 8 characters" (via `user_facing_password_error()`) - `Unexpected` -> "Something went wrong. Please try again." **Field association for `aria-describedby`** is handled in the template (`templates/register.html`): each error is rendered as a `

` with a specific `id` (e.g., `id="email-error"`, `id="password-error"`), and the corresponding input gets `aria-describedby="email-error" aria-invalid="true"` when the error is present. This pattern is documented in our glossary (`docs/glossary.md`, Error Copy Convention section) -- domain errors describe invariants for devs/logs, user-facing copy provides instructions for humans, and the route layer translates between them. **On your `UserFacingError` type idea:** I thought about this. The current approach uses the template struct fields directly (`email_error: Option`, `password_error: Option`, etc.) which implicitly binds errors to fields. A dedicated `UserFacingError { field: FieldId, message: String }` type would make this more explicit and reusable across forms. But for the MVP with only two forms (registration and login), the current approach is sufficient -- adding the abstraction would be premature. If we find ourselves duplicating the translation pattern across multiple forms, that's the signal to extract it. Sound right? The login route (`src/routes/login.rs`) follows the same pattern: `AuthenticationError::InvalidCredentials` -> "That email or password didn't work. Try again." (error copy from our glossary). The error there is a general form error (not field-specific), which is correct since we must not reveal which field is wrong.

content.text: Good -- Scott confirms the error translation layer is implemented correctly. His assessment on deferring the `UserFacingError` abstraction is right. Two forms don't justify the extraction.

input.content: Scott, agreed on all points. The translation layer in the route handler is correctly structured. Deferring the `UserFacingError` type until we have more forms is the right call -- extract when there's duplication, not before. One issue I flagged to Luca: the template currently renders error elements conditionally (only when an error exists). They need to always be in the DOM with `role="alert"`, empty when no error. This ensures `aria-describedby` always resolves and screen readers announce errors when they appear after HTMX form resubmission. The translation layer on the Rust side is fine -- it's the template rendering that needs the fix. Also: the `DuplicateEmail` message uses inline HTML (`signing in`) inside a `role="alert"` div. This works -- Steve Schoger asked and I confirmed links inside alert regions are announced correctly. The hardcoded HTML in the Rust handler (not user-interpolated) avoids XSS. Just noting this so we don't accidentally introduce user input into that pattern later.
input.summary: Agreed on all, template rendering fix needed

routing.summary: Agreed on all, template rendering fix needed
routing.content: Scott, agreed on all points. The translation layer in the route handler is correctly structured. Deferring the `UserFacingError` type until we have more forms is the right call -- extract when there's duplication, not before. One issue I flagged to Luca: the template currently renders error elements conditionally (only when an error exists). They need to always be in the DOM with `role="alert"`, empty when no error. This ensures `aria-describedby` always resolves and screen readers announce errors when they appear after HTMX form resubmission. The translation layer on the Rust side is fine -- it's the template rendering that needs the fix. Also: the `DuplicateEmail` message uses inline HTML (`signing in`) inside a `role="alert"` div. This works -- Steve Schoger asked and I confirmed links inside alert regions are announced correctly. The hardcoded HTML in the Rust handler (not user-interpolated) avoids XSS. Just noting this so we don't accidentally introduce user input into that pattern later.

content.text: Confirmed alignment with Scott on the error translation layer. The 4 accessibility issues I flagged on the registration template still need to be addressed. Standing by for CI to pass and for the team to pick up those fixes.
